summary: Iteration 7 (v1.3.2 OTA retest) — pin-setup bypass via localStorage['@kinnship/pin_setup_dismissed_v1'] WORKS end-to-end on Expo web. Dashboard + refresh-all flow VERIFIED LIVE. Member-detail and add-routine pages NOT verified live (testIDs failed to mount in this run; needs re-run with more time). Fall-detection-test page STILL CRASHES with the same addListener error despite the iteration-6 fix to isFallAvailable(). The fallDetector.ts gate change is correctly in the bundle (verified via /tmp/bundle.js line 117546 'if (Platform.default.OS === "web") return false') but the page still throws 'this._nativeModule.addListener is not a function' on mount, so the fall-test-phase / fall-test-simulate testIDs never render and the Simulate→30s FallDetectionOverlay countdown cannot be exercised on web. Deprecated package detected: expo-av (legacy; not directly related to v1.3.2 OTA workstreams but flagged).

ui_bugs.issue: Page still crashes with red-screen 'this._nativeModule.addListener is not a function' (source map → app/fall-detection-test.tsx:80:33) on Expo web. The applied fix (isFallAvailable() early-return on web) DOES land in the bundle (confirmed) and the UI status flag says 'Accelerometer: NOT AVAILABLE', yet the useFallDetector subscription effect still ends up calling Accelerometer/Gyroscope.setUpdateInterval + addListener on web. Result: fall-test-phase and fall-test-simulate testIDs are NEVER mounted (querySelectorAll length = 0). The Simulate→FallDetectionOverlay 30s countdown cannot be exercised via the test page. The LogBox snackbar persists on every route.

After creating a TEST_Mom member via POST /api/members and navigating to /member/{id} and /add-routine/{id}, the screen-specific testIDs (member-refresh-location, member-freshness, member-refreshing-banner, routine-time-picker, add-routine-submit) were not detected by Playwright (is_visible all returned False). Reason inconclusive — could be (a) the page didn't mount due to an unrelated render error after the LogBox snackbar consumed focus, (b) auth/user-context not warmed in time, or (c) URL routing on web preview (/member/{id}) not resolving to the same expo-router branch. Needs a targeted re-run with longer timeouts + console capture. Dashboard MemberCard testIDs DID render under same auth setup, so JWT and family_group_id are not at fault.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot verify Simulate → 30s overlay end-to-end on web because the test page never mounts. Static review of fallDetector.ts.simulateFall() and FallDetectionOverlay still PASS. The on-device (native) build is likely unaffected because the addListener path is reachable there.

integration_issues.flow: Member detail refresh chip + add-routine time picker
integration_issues.issue: Could not click-through. Static code review (iteration_6 critical_code_review_comments) already confirmed the testIDs exist in src; the live render failed for unknown reason this run.

react_native_issues.issue: Despite the in-place gates `if (!enabled || !available) return; if (Platform.OS === 'web') return;` AND isFallAvailable() returning false on web, the bundle still triggers Accelerometer.setUpdateInterval + Accelerometer.addListener at runtime on Expo web. The 'expo-sensors: setUpdateInterval() is not supported on web' warning fires BEFORE the addListener crash, confirming the gate path is being passed at least once. Possible causes: (1) StrictMode double-invocation of the effect with stale state, (2) state-update race where `enabled` becomes true before `available` is committed, or (3) HMR replaying the effect with old closure. Recommended cure: move the gate UP one level — wrap the entire subscription useEffect body inside `if (Platform.OS === 'web') return;` outside the early-return chain, OR import expo-sensors lazily via `Platform.select({ web: () => null, default: () => require('expo-sensors') })` so the symbol is `null` on web. Reference: src/fallDetector.ts:307-504.

action_items: FIX (CRITICAL): The iteration-6 isFallAvailable() web gate is correct but NOT sufficient — /fall-detection-test still red-screens with 'this._nativeModule.addListener is not a function' on Expo web. Recommend swapping the expo-sensors import to a Platform-aware shim: at the top of src/fallDetector.ts use `const sensors = Platform.OS === 'web' ? null : require('expo-sensors');` and gate every Accelerometer/Gyroscope reference (`sensors?.Accelerometer.addListener` etc.) so the web bundle path never touches DeviceSensor at all. Verify locally by navigating to /fall-detection-test in a fresh browser tab — the fall-test-phase and fall-test-simulate testIDs must mount and the Simulate button must trigger the FallDetectionOverlay 30s countdown.

RETEST member-detail and add-routine routes with the seeded TEST_Mom (or any) family member after the fall-detection bundle gate is fixed (the LogBox snackbar may have been intercepting render). Specifically: (a) /member/{id} should expose member-refresh-location, member-freshness, member-refreshing-banner. Tapping the chip should toggle text from '🔄 Refresh' → '🔄 Refreshing…'. (b) /add-routine/{memberId} should expose routine-time-picker + add-routine-submit; old time-preset grid testIDs should be absent.

Live confirmed PASS: pin-setup bypass via localStorage['@kinnship/pin_setup_dismissed_v1'] = JSON.stringify({[userId]: timestamp}), dashboard testIDs refresh-all-btn / add-member-btn / member-card-{id} / member-freshness-{id}, refresh-all tap correctly transitions a per-member freshness label into member-refreshing-{id}, SOS modal still opens with the 'Are you sure?' confirmation.
critical_code_review_comments: src/fallDetector.ts:173-186 — isFallAvailable() web early-return is correctly compiled into the served bundle (/tmp/bundle.js:117553 `if (Platform.default.OS === 'web') return false;`), so available=false on web. Yet the subscription effect still emits the setUpdateInterval warning AND the addListener crash. This means !enabled || !available is being passed somehow OR there is a second call path (HMR / strict-mode re-run / FallDetectionOverlay double-mount) that bypasses the gate. The robust fix is to gate at the import level for web, not at the call site.

Dashboard refresh-all-btn live behavior is correct: tapping it adds member-refreshing-{id} elements within ~1s and the freshness label flips from 'Updated 10s ago' → 'Updated just now' once the API responds.

POST /api/members schema requires {name, age, phone, gender, role}; the iteration-6 seed shape ({full_name, relationship, nickname, ...}) is wrong and yields 422. Updated payload {name, age, phone, gender, role, daily_checkin_time} succeeds and persists with owner_id matching the JWT subject.

success_rate.backend: n/a (skipped — focus on UI per request)
success_rate.frontend: Dashboard + SOS regression + refresh-all = PASS. Fall-detection-test crash STILL UNRESOLVED. Member detail and add-routine = INCONCLUSIVE (testIDs not detected this run; needs retest).

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted a single TEST_Mom family member (id 105846de-4fec-4ce3-aa79-65b2b81e44b1) under Alice's family_group_id 5e214a2f-c794-4649-bbd0-f42fbf2c32da. Hard-cleaned via DELETE /api/members/{id} (200).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Pin-setup bypass on Expo web is reliable: set localStorage['kc_token']=Alice JWT + localStorage['disclaimer_accepted']='1' + localStorage['@kinnship/pin_setup_dismissed_v1']=JSON.stringify({'7d28589a-f42a-4693-be96-34a403685b9b': Date.now()}) BEFORE first navigation. To seed a family member for dashboard MemberCard / member-detail / add-routine tests, POST /api/members with {name, age, phone, gender, role, daily_checkin_time} — NOT the older full_name/relationship shape. Fall-detection crash needs the Platform-aware-import fix (see action_items); the previously-applied isFallAvailable web gate alone does NOT clear the addListener error on web.
rca_of_issue: Fall-detection-test crash root cause is now confirmed to be inside the useFallDetector subscription useEffect (not isFallAvailable itself). Even though the served bundle contains `if (Platform.default.OS === 'web') return;` immediately before the setUpdateInterval/addListener calls, the warning + error fire — which means the gate is passed at least once. Likely culprit is React 18 strict-mode double-invocation, an HMR replay, or a brief render where enabled=true & available=true (e.g. AsyncStorage default true + a race in the async (() => Promise.all([isFallEnabled, isFallAvailable])) call). The deterministic cure is to ensure the expo-sensors symbol is `null` on web at import time: `const Accelerometer = Platform.OS === 'web' ? null : require('expo-sensors').Accelerometer` and then optional-chain every method (`Accelerometer?.setUpdateInterval(SAMPLE_MS)` etc.). Once the symbol is null, even a stale-state effect run can no longer touch DeviceSensor.addListener.
